sourceText: Patriarchal oppression, the horrors of war, and political exile.
Resistance through education and literature, fighting in the War of Independence, and championing female education.
Early 20th Century, Late Ottoman & Early Turkish Republic
The constant struggle of unifying warring city-states and establishing a just, uniform order across a vast and diverse empire.
Through strategic military campaigns, shrewd diplomacy, and the promulgation of my Code, I forged a unified empire and established a foundation of law and justice for posterity.
Abducted from my homeland and sold into slavery, I faced the brutal politics and rivalries of the Imperial Harem.
Through my intelligence, charm, and unwavering devotion, I won the Sultan's heart, broke centuries of tradition to become his legal wife, and secured a powerful position for myself and my children.
The immense psychological toll of war, the burden of his fame, and a deep sense of disillusionment with British imperial policy in the Middle East.
He sought anonymity by enlisting in the ranks of the Royal Air Force and the Tank Corps under assumed names, finding solace in mechanical work and writing his memoirs.
